name: Arkansas Farm Bureau
description_short: Arkansas Farm Bureau Federal Credit Union is a member-owned nonprofit credit union serving employees of Arkansas Farm Bureau and affiliated companies with savings accounts, loans, and financial services.
description_long: Arkansas Farm Bureau Federal Credit Union was organized by employees of Arkansas Farm Bureau and its associated companies to provide members with a cooperative financial institution focused on affordable credit and savings opportunities. As a nonprofit, member-owned credit union, the organization operates on principles of democratic control where members elect the volunteer board of directors and share in the institution's benefits through competitive rates and reasonable lending terms. The credit union offers a range of financial products including share savings accounts, one-year notice accounts (certificates), secured auto loans with rates starting at 3.99%, and signature loans up to $5,000. Members can also take advantage of semi-monthly payroll deductions for convenient savings and loan repayment. The credit union is NCUA-insured and maintains its main office in Little Rock, Arkansas, with traditional business hours Monday through Friday from 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM. Membership eligibility is limited to regular full-time employees of Arkansas Farm Bureau and associated companies, with a nominal $1.00 membership fee. The institution emphasizes member safety, reasonable rates, and the cooperative principle of pooling funds to serve member needs at fair terms.
services: Share savings accounts with competitive yields
Semi-monthly payroll deductions for loan repayment
Member election of board of directors
pros: Auto loan rates as low as 3.99% for new and used vehicles
Share savings accounts with competitive yields and no deposit/withdrawal limitations
Competitive dividend rates on One-Year Notice Accounts for longer-term savings
Signature loans available up to $5,000 for member needs
Semi-monthly payroll deduction options for savings and loan repayment convenience
Nonprofit, member-owned structure ensures democratic control and member benefit focus
Minimal membership fee of only $1.00 for eligible employees
cons: Membership restricted to regular full-time employees of Arkansas Farm Bureau and affiliated companies only
Limited service hours (Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM-4:30 PM) with no evening or weekend availability
Signature loans capped at $5,000, limiting access to larger unsecured credit
Online banking interface appears basic with limited digital service details provided on website
Single physical office location in Little Rock limits in-person accessibility for geographically distant members
best_for: Full-time Arkansas Farm Bureau employees seeking affordable auto financing and savings vehicles
Members looking for cooperative financial services with member-owned governance and fair pricing
Employees wanting convenient payroll deduction options for savings and loan management
diagnosis: Arkansas Farm Bureau FCU is best for regular full-time employees of Arkansas Farm Bureau and affiliated companies seeking member-owned cooperative banking with competitive auto loan rates and savings options. The main caveat is that membership is restricted to employees of specific organizations, making it inaccessible to the general public, and the institution offers limited loan amounts and basic digital services compared to larger financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
